_comment: Declarative CRUD lifecycles. Steps run in order: create -> read -> [update] -> delete. {placeholders} are filled from earlier 'capture' values plus {unique} (collision-free token) and {region}. Mutating steps need SCP_ALLOW_MUTATIONS=true; steps marked destructive also need SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE=true. A step may declare "wait": <s> (sleep before it) or "poll": {field, until, timeout, interval} to wait for async provisioning. Enable a lifecycle with "enabled": true.

lifecycles._note: Registry DELETE returns 500 for a few minutes right after creation (provisioning race), then succeeds; the delete step retries 500 up to ~6 min. Enabled.
Light, self-contained: issue a self-signed certificate, read it, list, delete. Synchronous.
Light, self-contained: create a Standard queue, read it, list, delete. Synchronous.
Light, self-contained: security groups are account/region-scoped (no VPC needed, per validated VM/ske lifecycles). Create SG + an ingress rule, read, list, delete both.

Light, zero-cost, self-contained: create an SSH keypair (public_key omitted so SCP generates one), read by name, list, probe, delete. Synchronous. VALIDATED-FACT create body (matches the VM lifecycle's keypair step).

Light, no-VPC: create a block volume, snapshot it, probe-read both (showvolume + showsnapshot), then tear down. Volume create body mirrors the VM lifecycle's create-volume (VALIDATED). Snapshot create body grounded from docs (createsnapshot). Captures use $.id (volume id convention confirmed from the VM lifecycle); snapshot id inferred as $.id — regrsnap* swept as insurance.
Light, self-contained: allocate a public IP (type IGW, unattached), read, list, probe, delete. Best-effort create body from docs (createpublicip 1.2); async create -> $.publicip.id.
Light, self-contained: create a VPC inline, attach an internet gateway, read both, probe, tear down in reverse. Best-effort create body from docs (createinternetgateway 1.2); $.internet_gateway.id.

VALIDATED end-to-end (real K8s cluster + nodepool, ~27min, billable). v1.4 schema: volume_id is a string, service_watch_logging_enabled required; nodepool with SSD_Provisioned needs volume_max_iops/throughput. SLOW + costly; disable for routine runs if needed. | EXPANDED (best-guess, validate via heavy dispatch): nodepool scale up/down + label set/clear.

Core create/read/delete path VALIDATED end-to-end (creates a real, billable VM ~17min and tears it down). Enabled per request; disable (enabled=false) to avoid recurring VM cost on routine runs. EXPANSION (NOT yet dispatch-validated — confirm on next HEAVY run): read captures capture-server-port ($.interfaces[0].id) / capture-server-volume ($.volumes[0].id) are SOFT (probe-only; a miss skips the dependent probe, never fails the run) and feed a 13-GET probe; write op rename-server (PUT /v1/servers/{id}) confirmed to require a 'name' matching ^[a-zA-Z0-9-_ ]*$ (tags are NOT updatable via this endpoint); stop/start power-cycle VALIDATED (STOPPED/SHUTOFF/ACTIVE states correct). Extra block-volume re-added: create standalone /v1/volumes (volume_type:'SSD', size:104 int, capture $.id, poll $.state 'available'), attach via POST /v1/servers/{server_id}/volumes (volume_id+device /dev/vdb), then detach + delete on teardown. The field is volume_type NOT type.

HEAVY: provisions a real, billable MySQL DBaaS cluster (single-node, ha_enabled=false) on its own VPC/subnet, waits until service_state RUNNING, then tears everything down. Slow (~20-40min). Runs only via dispatch run_heavy=true. v1.1 schema: server_type_name (name, not id) + dbaas_engine_version_id from list endpoints; service_ip_address must sit inside the subnet CIDR. | EXPANDED (best-guess bodies/JSONPaths, validate via heavy dispatch).

Shared-infra parallel heavy plan: ONE vpc+subnet, then 5 dbaas engines (mysql/postgresql/mariadb/epas/cachestore); VM excluded (#6 no identifiable boot image); eventstreams (undocumented topology value_error), searchengine/sqlserver/vertica (require a real license) excluded. Subnet/vpc teardown retries 409 to wait out async cluster termination. created CONCURRENTLY (creates fired back-to-back), waited, probe-read, torn down. Each is an optional group: a bad body skips only that group (cleaned up). Bodies grounded per-engine from api_bodies.json. Service IPs .11-.19 (db) in the shared subnet; VM uses its own sg/keypair. UNVALIDATED per-group until a heavy run surfaces warnings (the runner warns per skipped group with status+body).

DISABLED pending a private-dns activation sub-flow. Real bodies now known (framework/api_bodies.json): createhostedzone requires private_dns_id (+type:'private'), which comes from createprivatedns ($.private_dns.id) followed by activateprivatedns and an active-state wait; record create $.id, zone create $.id (bare envelope, not $.hosted_zone.id). Re-enable once the private-dns create+activate+wait chain is built.

Real body from API ref (security/kms/createkey, see framework/api_bodies.json). Self-contained: create transit key -> get -> list -> probe path-param GETs -> delete (schedule deletion). Response envelope $.key.id confirmed from the doc response example.

Real bodies from API ref (createsecretsmanager + createkey). The secret requires a kms_id, so the lifecycle creates a KMS key first (chain) and tears it down last. secret_value must be a JSON STRING; private_acl_enabled is a string 'false'. Captures $.secret.id / $.key.id confirmed from doc response examples. Secret DELETE requires a body {waiting_time_ndays} (recovery window) — sent on both the delete step and the cleanup.

Light, VPC-FREE, self-contained. Creates an API (REGION endpoint), reads root resource id, creates a child resource, creates a deployment, probe-reads every {api_id} GET, tears down. Create body grounded from docs createapi 1.3 ({name,description,endpoint_type:REGION,tags:[{key,value}],id:''}; 202 -> $.id). A new API auto-creates a root resource, recovered via listresources (200 -> $.resources[0].id) and used as createresource parent_id. createresource path is /v1/apis/{api_id}/resources/{parent_id} with body {path_part} (200 -> $.id). createapideployment POST /v1/apis/{api_id}/deployments (200 -> $.deployment_id) exercises listapideployments. regrapi{unique} is lowercase-alnum (safe). create-deployment removed: deploying requires >=1 API method (NoMethodsExist); not needed for the api/resource GETs, and listapideployments is already covered via api_id.

Light, VPC-FREE, self-contained. Creates a Cloud Function (inline content, Node.js:20 runtime), waits for state READY, then attaches a CRONJOB trigger (no apigateway/VPC dependency), probe-reads every {cloud_function_id} + {trigger_id} GET, and tears down. Create body grounded from docs createcloudfunction 1.4 ({content,name,runtime,tags}; 202 -> $.id, $.state READY). showcloudfunction wraps in $.cloud_function.state. Cronjob trigger grounded from createcloudfunctioncronjobtrigger 1.4 (200 -> $.triggers[0].trigger_id; schedule is 5-field cron, timezone tz name). Trigger delete is DELETE /v1/triggers/{trigger_id}. regrscf{unique} name is lowercase-alnum (safe). tags shape is [{key,value}].

steps.name: delete-trigger
steps._note: body is a best-guess (doc exposes no params); 400 tolerated since the 12 GETs are captured by probe-reads before teardown and the leftover-sweep removes any orphaned trigger.

lifecycles._note: ONE shared vpc(10.140.0.0/20)+subnet(10.140.1.0/24)+publicip, then networking families created back-to-back as optional groups (igw, port, natgw, tgw, subnetvip, lb chain hc->sg->lb->listener->members, dns chain privatedns->activate->zone->record), probe-read per service, torn down children-first. A bad body skips only its group (cleaned up; runner warns with status+body). Bodies grounded from framework/api_bodies.json. Skipped (need backing/external resource): privatelink-service/-endpoint (need backing LB+approval), vpc-peering (cross-account approval), vpc-endpoint (needs a privatelink service), private-nat (separate flow), lb-certificate (needs an uploaded cert), public-domain-name (real paid domain registration order). layer_type:L4 and several LB enum guesses UNVALIDATED until a heavy run surfaces warnings.

Light, self-contained: create an IAM group (no path-param POST /v1/groups), read it, probe the 3 group-id path-param GETs, delete. Body grounded on management/iam/creategroup example (api_bodies.json); policy_ids/user_ids sent empty so no pre-existing principals are needed. Create response envelope $.group.id confirmed from the doc 201 example. {ualpha} = letters-only unique token (group names in the example are alphabetic).

Light, self-contained: create a USER_DEFINED identity policy (no path-param POST /v1/policies), read it, probe the 2 policy-id path-param GETs, delete. Body grounded on management/iam/createpolicy example; server-assigned id fields (default_version_id, policy_version.policy_id/policy_version_id) are OMITTED — they are returned by the API, not supplied on create. policy_document mirrors the example verbatim (Version 2024-07-01). Create response envelope is FLAT: $.id (confirmed from the doc 201 example).

Light, self-contained: create a USER_DEFINED role (no path-param POST /v1/roles), read it, probe the 2 role-id path-param GETs, delete. Body grounded on management/iam/createrole example; assume_role_policy_document copied verbatim (sts:AssumeRole). policy_ids/principals sent empty to avoid referencing non-existent users. Create response envelope $.role.id confirmed from the doc 202 example. RISK: the trust-policy / principals validation is account-specific — if the gateway requires a real principal SRN, create returns 400 and this lifecycle should be disabled; everything else is grounded.
lifecycles._disabled_reason: create POST /v1/roles -> 500 ContactAdminForAssistance (backend); covers only showrole/listrolepolicybindings. Revisit.

lifecycles._note: Light child-resource lifecycle: create log group -> create log stream under it -> read both -> probe path-param GETs (covers showloggroup AND showloggrouplogstream) -> delete stream -> delete group. Bodies grounded from createloggroup / createloggrouplogstream; envelopes $.log_group.id and $.log_stream.id / $.log_stream.log_group_id confirmed from doc responses.
